agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Go down the hall past some plants towards a doorway to the hot tub and some lounge chairs.  Stop in front of the large tub. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.

messages.content: I'm right at the doorway now. Through it I can see the blue-lit room. The center clearance is 1.9m. Let me move forward through the doorway into the hot tub room.

message.content: I'm right at the doorway now. Through it I can see the blue-lit room. The center clearance is 1.9m. Let me move forward through the doorway into the hot tub room.

message.content: I'm right at the doorway threshold now (center clearance 0.9m - the doorframe). Through the doorway I can see the blue-lit hot tub room. Let me move forward through the doorway.

message.content: I'm now inside the hot tub room. I can see lounge chairs on the left and what appears to be the pool/tub area with blue lighting. Let me look around to locate the large tub clearly.

messages.content: The "ahead" view shows the hot tub room with lounge chairs on the left and the tub/pool area ahead with blue lighting. There's a wall/pillar to my immediate left. Let me move forward into the room toward the tub area. The ahead direction has good clearance in center (3.2m).

message.content: The "ahead" view shows the hot tub room with lounge chairs on the left and the tub/pool area ahead with blue lighting. There's a wall/pillar to my immediate left. Let me move forward into the room toward the tub area. The ahead direction has good clearance in center (3.2m).

messages.content: Now I can see the room more clearly. There are lounge chairs on the left and the large tub/pool area in the center-right with the blue lighting. The tub is ahead of me. Let me move toward the large tub. It looks like it's ahead and slightly to the right.

message.content: Now I can see the room more clearly. There are lounge chairs on the left and the large tub/pool area in the center-right with the blue lighting. The tub is ahead of me. Let me move toward the large tub. It looks like it's ahead and slightly to the right.

messages.content: I can see the large tub/pool clearly now - it's the wide basin structure in the center-right of the view. There are lounge chairs on the left. Let me move a bit closer to the tub and slightly right to face it.

message.content: I can see the large tub/pool clearly now - it's the wide basin structure in the center-right of the view. There are lounge chairs on the left. Let me move a bit closer to the tub and slightly right to face it.

messages.content: Now I have a clear view of the large hot tub directly ahead of me. It's the large tiled basin structure in the center of the view. The clearance is about 3.5m to it. Let me move forward a bit more to get within 3 meters and be right in front of it.

message.content: Now I have a clear view of the large hot tub directly ahead of me. It's the large tiled basin structure in the center of the view. The clearance is about 3.5m to it. Let me move forward a bit more to get within 3 meters and be right in front of it.

The center clearance reads 3.8m but that's likely measuring the far wall past the tub since the tub rim is lower. I'm very close to the tub edge now. message.content: I'm now right in front of the large hot tub. It fills the view ahead of me - the large tiled tub structure. Let me move forward just slightly to be right in front of it.

messages.content:
I am now directly in front of the large hot tub. The tub occupies the lower center of my view and I'm right at its edge. This matches the instruction perfectly: I went down the hall past plants, through the doorway into the hot tub room with lounge chairs, and I'm now stopped in front of the large tub.

I'll issue STOP now.

message.content:
I am now directly in front of the large hot tub. The tub occupies the lower center of my view and I'm right at its edge. This matches the instruction perfectly: I went down the hall past plants, through the doorway into the hot tub room with lounge chairs, and I'm now stopped in front of the large tub.

I'll issue STOP now.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 76 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 24, "tool_calls_remaining": 76}
